title: Unnamed variable declarations should use the "var" identifier
description: In Java, unnamed variable declarations should use the "var" identifier to improve code clarity and maintainability. This rule helps ensure that unnamed variables are declared in a consistent and modern way, leveraging Java's type inference capabilities.
recommendation: Use the 'var' identifier for unnamed variable declarations instead of explicit types.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability by encouraging modern Java practices.

noncompliant:
int _ = someMethod(); // Noncompliant: should use 'var'
String _ = "value"; // Noncompliant: should use 'var'

checks.message: Use the 'var' identifier for unnamed variable declarations instead of explicit types.
